resume.summary: Motivated B.Tech Computer Science student with a strong interest in network security, ethical hacking, and vulnerability assessment. Hands-on experience with penetration testing tools and secure coding practices. Seeking a Cyber Security Internship to contribute to real-world security operations.

education.school: Dr. Vishwanath Karad MIT World Peace University, Pune
education.coursework: Relevant Coursework: Network Security, Cryptography, Ethical Hacking, Digital Forensics, Operating Systems

technicalSkills.skillGroup: Nmap, Wireshark, Burp Suite, Metasploit, OWASP ZAP
Network Security, Vulnerability Assessment, Penetration Testing, Cryptography
Linux, Kali Linux, Git, GitHub, AWS (basic)

experience.title: Cyber Security Intern — Synapse Analytics Pvt. Ltd., Pune

bullets.bullet: Performed vulnerability assessments on internal web applications, identifying 12 medium/high-risk issues.
Assisted in configuring firewall and IDS rules, reducing false-positive alerts by 20%.
Documented security findings and remediation steps in formal reports for the engineering team.
Set up a vulnerable web app lab and performed testing against OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities.
Identified and documented SQL injection and XSS vulnerabilities with proof-of-concept exploits.
Proposed mitigations including input validation and parameterized queries.
Built a rule-based IDS to detect port scans and ARP spoofing in real time.
Achieved 90% detection accuracy on simulated attack traffic.
Built a CLI tool evaluating password strength and checking for known data breaches.
Used secure hashing practices to avoid storing plaintext credentials.

achievements.achievement: Selected among 80 students college-wide for the Smart India Hackathon (SIH 2024) internal round.
Received the Academic Excellence Award for Semester 2 with the highest GPA (9.3) in the CSE department cohort of 120 students.
Active contributor to two college-wide technical fests, coordinating logistics and technical sessions for 150+ participants.

extracurricular.activity: Core Member, Technical Club, VIT Pune — organised hands-on workshops for 150+ students (2023–Present).
Technical Blogger — published articles on Medium covering tools and best practices relevant to the field; collectively read 4,000+ times.
Volunteer Mentor, CodeForGood (NGO Initiative) — guided 15 high school students through technology fundamentals over an 8-week programme.
